findings.evidence: `Cylinder.lean:73` shows `blockLaw_blockCylinder {μ : Measure Ω}` has no finite-measure assumption; Mathlib `Bochner/Set.lean:185` has `integral_indicator₀ (hs : NullMeasurableSet s μ)` and `Bochner/Set.lean:527` has `setIntegral_const ... = μ.real s • c`, both without `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]`.
findings.fix: Drop `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]` from the theorem statement; keep the existing `hX` and `hC` hypotheses.
findings.issue: `integral_blockIndicatorProd` assumes `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]`, but that hypothesis is stronger than the natural statement and is not used by the proof ingredients.

summary: The new theorem has an unnecessary finite-measure assumption; the underlying indicator integral identity and block-law rectangle lemma already work for arbitrary measures with the same `toReal` convention.
